steps.subject: Trade referrals? We send you work, you send us work.
steps.body:
Hi {first_name},

I'm Tal with Goldman's Garage Door Repair in {city}. I came across {company_name} and thought this might be a good fit.

We get calls all the time from homeowners who also need {trade_type} work. Right now we don't have a go-to person to send them to, so those referrals go nowhere.

Here's what I'm thinking — simple two-way referral setup:

- When our customers need a {trade_type}, we send them your way
- When your customers mention a garage door issue, you send them ours
- No contracts, no fees, just real job volume for both of us

Interested? Happy to grab a quick call or just keep it to text/email.

Tal Goldman
Goldman's Garage Door Repair
{phone}

steps.subject: Re: Trade referrals between {company_name} and Goldman's
steps.body:
Hey {first_name},

Just circling back on the referral idea. We're doing 15-20 jobs a week in {city} and {nearby_city}, and homeowners regularly ask if we know a good {trade_type}.

Right now we just say "check Google" — which isn't great for anyone. I'd rather send them to someone I trust, and it sounds like {company_name} does solid work based on your reviews.

No commitment needed. If you get a customer who mentions their garage door, just text me their info. I'll do the same when {trade_type} comes up.

Worth a shot?

Tal
{phone}

steps.subject: Open invite, {first_name}
steps.body:
Hey {first_name},

Last note from me on this — I won't keep bugging you. The referral offer stands whenever it makes sense.

If you ever want to swap referrals, just shoot me a text. No forms, no contracts. Just two local businesses helping each other out in {city}.

Hope business is good.

Tal Goldman
Goldman's Garage Door Repair
{phone}

notes: Send from MI number for MI leads, NC number for NC leads. {trade_type} should be populated with their actual trade (HVAC, electrician, plumber, painter, handyman, locksmith, landscaper).
